entities.name: śabda
entities.cite: the word's own form is ascertained

entities.name: svarūpa
entities.cite: the settled ascertainment of the word's svarupa

entities.name: avadharaṇa
entities.cite: and it terminates in avadharana, the settled ascertainment of the word's svarupa

entities.name: grahaṇa
entities.cite: the *ripened grasp* seizes one undivided thing

entities.name: sphota
entities.cite: This gives the sphota theory its account of process without surrendering the unity of the object

entities.name: grahaṇa-anuguna
entities.cite: they are grahana-anuguna, conducive to the grasp

axioms.name: anupakhyeya
axioms.cite: The intermediate cognitions in learning — each pass through the verse, each stroke of sound — are anupakhyeya, not separately reportable

axioms.name: grahana_anuguna
axioms.cite: But they are grahana-anuguna, conducive to the grasp

axioms.name: terminates_in
axioms.cite: their work is real though silent, and it terminates in avadharana, the settled ascertainment of the word's svarupa

axioms.name: avadharana_of
axioms.cite: and it terminates in avadharana, the settled ascertainment of the word's svarupa

axioms.name: prakasayati
axioms.cite: once the sound has illuminated it

axioms.name: ekam_advayam
axioms.cite: the *ripened grasp* seizes one undivided thing

axioms.name: avirbhavati_yugapat
axioms.cite: comprehension does in fact arrive as an all-at-once click after a subpersonal accumulation

denials.name: samkalitaprakara
denials.cite: not as a felt summation of parts

claims.name: anupakhyeya
claims.cite: by cognitions that cannot be separately reported

claims.name: grahana_anuguna
claims.cite: yet are conducive to the final grasp

claims.name: avadharana_of
claims.cite: the word's own form is ascertained

claims.name: prakasayati
claims.cite: once the sound has illuminated it

rejected_readings.label: pratyaya_as_final_cognition
rejected_readings.rendering: The intermediate cognitions directly constitute the final ascertainment of the word's form.
rejected_readings.why: This reading conflates pratyaya with avadharana, collapsing the careful distinction the commentary maintains: pratyaya are not separately reportable intermediate steps, while avadharana is their unified culmination. The commentary explicitly marks avadharana as a distinct termination point.

rejected_readings.label: dhvani_is_svarupa
rejected_readings.rendering: The sound itself is the word's form.
rejected_readings.why: The commentary distinguishes the gradual, many-staged process of sound strokes from the unified svarupa they illuminate. Conflating them ignores the phenomenology: the undivided thing that comprehension seizes is not identical to the accumulation of phonemes.

rejected_readings.label: summation_view
rejected_readings.rendering: The ascertainment of the word's form is a consciously experienced addition of successive cognitions.
rejected_readings.why: The commentary explicitly rejects this: comprehension arrives as an all-at-once click, not as a felt summation of parts. This reading misses the key phenomenological claim about the discontinuity between gradual ripening and instantaneous grasp.

notes: The contract captures the sphota doctrine's central move: how intermediate cognitions that are individually unreportable can still conduct to a unified, all-at-once grasp of the word's form. The six sorts fit the ontological layers: linguistic items (sound, word, unified sphota), properties (the form and reportability-status of those items), cognitions (the graded accumulation and its final culmination), and power (the ripening process itself). The translation provides the accepted reading's cites; the commentary provides the axioms and denials that ground the theory.
